code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test03-MAR-2017Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low CO₂, high chloride, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and mild ketonuria in urine, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). These findings suggest impaired renal function, likely with tubular involvement (possible acute or chronic kidney disease), and possible metabolic acidosis. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes such as diabetes or glomerular disease.
